_comment: Braced-charge demo: a lone player Infantry unit spawns ALREADY in Shield Wall (formation 4, set at spawn -- see Battle._spawn_scenario's `formation` field), facing down, and is charged HEAD-ON by a single cavalry that spawns in front of it (below; team 1 advances upward into it). Starting pre-formed keeps the clip focused on the one thing it's meant to show -- the soldier-level contact-impulse physics arresting the charger's momentum -- instead of spending frames on the Shield Wall keybind gesture, which is a different, already-shown feature (see anti-cav-square.json, shared with the website's own 'square' demo). Unlike anti-cav-square.json's two-cavalry REAR charge (which drags the defender's regiment facing back and forth between two attackers), this is a single frontal charge: the regiment never needs to re-face, so the clip is a clean showcase with no spinning-formation artifact. Deterministic (scenario + seed + scripted input, no player gesture needed).
